title: Modal Verbs â€” muset, moct, smÄ›t
description: Learn to conjugate the three Czech modal verbs â€” muset (must), moct (can/be able to), and smÄ›t (may/be allowed to) â€” and use them with infinitives in present-tense sentences.

exercises.prompt: What is the 'jĂˇ' (1st person singular) form of 'muset' (must)?

data.question_en: What is the 'jĂˇ' (1st person singular) form of 'muset' (must)?
data.explanation: 'Muset' is an -et class verb. The 1st person singular is 'musĂ­m'. Conjugation: musĂ­m, musĂ­Ĺˇ, musĂ­, musĂ­me, musĂ­te, musĂ­.

exercises.prompt: What is the 'ty' (2nd person singular) form of 'moct' (can)?

data.question_en: What is the 'ty' (2nd person singular) form of 'moct' (can)?
data.explanation: 'Moct' is irregular. The 2nd person singular is 'mĹŻĹľeĹˇ'. Full conjugation: mohu/mĹŻĹľu, mĹŻĹľeĹˇ, mĹŻĹľe, mĹŻĹľeme, mĹŻĹľete, mohou/mĹŻĹľou.

exercises.prompt: What is the 'oni' (3rd person plural) form of 'smÄ›t' (may/be allowed)?

data.question_en: What is the 'oni' (3rd person plural) form of 'smÄ›t' (may/be allowed)?
data.explanation: 'SmÄ›t' is an -et class verb. The 3rd person plural is 'smÄ›jĂ­'. Conjugation: smĂ­m, smĂ­Ĺˇ, smĂ­, smĂ­me, smĂ­te, smÄ›jĂ­.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the correct form of 'muset' for 'my' (we).

data.explanation: 'My musĂ­me jĂ­t do Ĺˇkoly.' = We must go to school. The 1st person plural of 'muset' is 'musĂ­me'. The modal verb is followed by the infinitive 'jĂ­t'.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the correct form of 'moct' for 'on' (he).

data.explanation: 'On mĹŻĹľe mluvit ÄŤesky.' = He can speak Czech. The 3rd person singular of 'moct' is 'mĹŻĹľe'. Modal verbs take the infinitive 'mluvit' after them.

exercises.prompt: Translate to Czech: 'I must work tomorrow.'

data.grammar_note: 'MusĂ­m' = I must (1st person singular of 'muset'). The infinitive 'pracovat' follows the modal. Word order is flexible: 'MusĂ­m zĂ­tra pracovat' or 'ZĂ­tra musĂ­m pracovat' are both correct.

exercises.prompt: Translate to English: 'Ona smĂ­ jĂ­t ven.'

accepted_answers: She is allowed to go out.
data.grammar_note: 'SmĂ­' = may/is allowed to (3rd person singular of 'smÄ›t'). 'JĂ­t ven' = to go out. 'SmÄ›t' specifically means permission, not ability.

exercises.prompt: Arrange the words to form: 'You can help us.'

data.explanation: 'Ty mĹŻĹľeĹˇ nĂˇm pomoci.' = You can help us. 'MĹŻĹľeĹˇ' = you can (2nd person singular of 'moct'). 'Pomoci' = to help (infinitive). The modal verb 'mĹŻĹľeĹˇ' is followed by the infinitive 'pomoci'.

exercises.prompt: Match each modal verb to its English meaning.

data.prompt_en: Match each Czech modal verb to its English meaning.
data.explanation: 'Muset' = must (obligation), 'moct' = can (ability/possibility), 'smÄ›t' = may (permission). These are the three Czech modal verbs, each with a distinct meaning.

exercises.prompt: Match each conjugated form to the correct pronoun.

data.prompt_en: Match each conjugated form of 'moct' to the correct pronoun.
data.explanation: 'Moct' conjugation: jĂˇ mohu/mĹŻĹľu, ty mĹŻĹľeĹˇ, on/ona mĹŻĹľe, my mĹŻĹľeme, vy mĹŻĹľete, oni mohou/mĹŻĹľou. Note the stem change from 'moh-' to 'mĹŻĹľ-' in most forms.

exercises.prompt: Find the mistake in this sentence.

data.prompt_en: Find the mistake in this sentence using modal verbs.
data.explanation: The 2nd person singular of 'muset' is 'musĂ­Ĺˇ', not 'musĂ­'. 'MusĂ­' is the 3rd person singular (on/ona musĂ­) and 3rd person plural (oni musĂ­). 'Ty musĂ­Ĺˇ jĂ­t domĹŻ.' = You must go home.

exercises.prompt: Find the mistake in this sentence.

data.prompt_en: Find the mistake â€” wrong modal verb used.
data.explanation: 'NesmĂ­Ĺˇ' means 'you must not' (prohibition), while 'nemusĂ­Ĺˇ' means 'you don't have to' (no obligation). The correct sentence is 'NemusĂ­Ĺˇ jĂ­t, pokud nechceĹˇ.' = You don't have to go if you don't want to. This is a common confusion between 'muset' and 'smÄ›t'.
